content.problemStatement:
Given three strings `s1`, `s2`, and `s3`, write a recursive function to determine if `s3` can be obtained by interleaving `s1` and `s2`. An interleaving of two strings `s1` and `s2` is a configuration where they are merged in such a way that maintains the left-to-right order of characters from each string.


Input Format

First line of input contains the string `s1`.
Second line of input contains the string `s2`.
Third line of input contains the string `s3`.


Output Format

Output `True` if `s3` can be obtained by interleaving `s1` and `s2`, else `False`.
constraints: All strings contain only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, three lines follow: the first line is string s1, the second line is string s2, and the third line is string s3. All strings consist of lowercase English letters and may be empty. The length of s3 is guaranteed to be |s1|+|s2|.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "True" if s3 can be formed by interleaving s1 and s2 while preserving the order of characters from each string, otherwise output "False".

sampleTestCases.explanation: s3 can be formed by taking a from s1, d from s2, b from s1, c from s1, e from s2, f from s2, preserving order.
No way to interleave s1 and s2 to obtain s3 while keeping the relative order of characters.
All strings are empty, so s3 is trivially an interleaving of s1 and s2.

solution.approach: We use dynamic programming. Let dp[i][j] be true if the first i characters of s1 and the first j characters of s2 can interleave to form the first i+j characters of s3. The transition is: if i>0 and s1[i-1]==s3[i+j-1] and dp[i-1][j] is true then dp[i][j]=true; similarly, if j>0 and s2[j-1]==s3[i+j-1] and dp[i][j-1] is true then dp[i][j]=true. Initialise dp[0][0]=true and fill the table row‑wise. The answer is dp[|s1|][|s2|]. This runs in O(|s1|·|s2|) time and O(|s1|·|s2|) memory per test case, which easily satisfies the constraints.
